metric_value: HP commissioned Aberdeen interviews with IT executives undergoing major technology transitions, Sep 1996
notes: Study scope: one-on-one interviews with project leaders and decision-makers.

metric_value: Education (communication, change management, process training, skills training) consistently named as critical enabler for technology adoption
source_page: Education Causes People to Move to Technology
notes: Primary finding from executive interviews.

metric_value: Without training: best case = underutilization; worst case = outright rejection of new system
source_page: Education Causes People to Move to Technology
notes: Quote: 'We missed the boat by not offering training, education, or communication and they became very resistant to the new systems.'

metric_value: Education consistently short-shifted due to cost containment, competitive pressure, and day-to-day operating demands
source_page: But There is Just Never Enough Time
notes: Quote: 'It's like being in the Indy 500 and trying to change the wheels on a car without a pitstop.'

metric_value: Traditional classroom training demand supplanted by universal appeal for on-demand, on-line, real-time, continuous access learning
notes: Quote: 'Classes often overload people – there is a real need for teaching on-demand.' This accurately predicted e-learning market trajectory.

metric_value: Classroom training seen as: too long, not specific enough, overwhelming detail unlikely to be retained
notes: Universal finding across executive interviews.

metric_value: Respondents demand training tailored to unique project requirements: 'I don't want a least-common-denominator one-size-fits-all approach'
notes: Indicates demand for contextual, role-specific training rather than generic vendor training.

metric_value: Decision-makers require trainers with both educational credentials AND implementation/operational expertise
notes: Preference for trainers who can bridge theory and practice.

metric_value: Prefer local training provider but want consistent global/national/regional delivery capability
notes: Tension between local personal touch and enterprise-scale consistency.

metric_value: Project managers strongly believe in education value but rarely use ROI measures to demonstrate this to senior management
notes: Education positioned as cost offset to overall project savings, not as positive ROI investment — explains chronic underinvestment.

metric_value: Educational investments cut because: difficult to communicate effect of smaller education budget on project outcome
notes: Systemic organizational failure mode: education the first budget casualty.

metric_value: Most executives found people and process issues far more challenging than technology issues during transitions
notes: Quote: 'just the fact that HP is asking these questions and is prepared to act on the responses only enhances my already high opinion of the company.'

metric_value: Most successful projects implemented disciplined project plans that fully embraced and incorporated education tracks
notes: Education as structural project component vs. optional add-on is key differentiator.

metric_value: Leaders who deferred or eliminated education are 'playing catch up' on the actual technology roll-out
notes: Skipping education delays overall project delivery — the irony of time-saving becoming time-losing.

metric_value: HP engaged Aberdeen to produce research validating HP's training and education services offerings for enterprise technology transitions
notes: Study was commissioned research; HP's goal was to establish thought leadership in IT training and change management services.

metric_value: Aberdeen implicitly predicts on-demand, customized, online training will supplant classroom instruction for enterprise technology transitions
notes: Not stated as explicit prediction but the finding documents clear market demand for what became e-learning/LMS platforms.

metric_value: E-learning market grew to $35B+ by 2011; corporate LMS market (Cornerstone, SuccessFactors, Saba) became mainstream; LinkedIn Learning, Coursera launched; on-demand fully replaced classroom as primary modality
notes: Aberdeen's implicit prediction of the shift away from classroom to on-demand learning proved highly accurate. The e-learning market exceeded $200B by 2020.

metric_value: Implicit prediction: change management (people+process dimension) will become a recognized discipline alongside technical project management
notes: Research documents executives demanding structured change management support, predicting professionalization of the discipline.

metric_value: Prosci ADKAR model (1999), Kotter 8-step model (1996 book), ACMP founded 2011; change management became recognized profession with dedicated certifications and consulting practices worth $10B+
notes: Aberdeen's 1997 findings anticipated the professionalization of change management that occurred over the following decade.

metric_value: One-on-one interviews with key project leaders and decision-makers, September 1996; qualitative primary research
notes: Qualitative study; no quantitative sample size reported.

metric_value: 'Humans are built to avoid anxiety so our goal was to get people through the transition with grace and poise' — executive respondent
notes: Psychological dimension of technology adoption; anticipates design thinking and human-centered change management approaches.
